title: gh-148820: Fix _PyRawMutex use-after-free on spurious semaphore wakeup
category: lock/semaphore wakeup race use-after-free (_PyRawMutex stack semaphore freed before wakeup completes)
crash_site: Fatal error / use-after-free in the semaphore wakeup: the unlocking thread calls _PySemaphore_Wakeup() on a waiter's semaphore that the waiter already destroyed. Surfaces as a crash inside the semaphore primitive touching freed (stack-allocated) memory, under raw-mutex contention with interrupts.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: _PySemaphore_Wakeup / _PySemaphore_Wait / parking_lot primitives (they are correct); the CAS that removes the waiter from the list. The fix actually edits _PyRawMutex_LockSlow (the waiter side), but the unsafe pattern originates in _PyRawMutex_UnlockSlow's wakeup-without-handshake.
ground_truth.fix_summary: _PyRawMutex_UnlockSlow CAS-removes a waiter then calls _PySemaphore_Wakeup() with no handshake. If that waiter's _PySemaphore_Wait() returned Py_PARK_INTR (interrupted), the waiter destroys its stack-allocated semaphore before the unlocker's wakeup runs -> use-after-free / fatal error. Fix: _PyRawMutex_LockSlow now loops until _PySemaphore_Wait() returns Py_PARK_OK (it must observe a real wakeup, not exit on INTR while a wakeup may still be pending against its semaphore).

admission.note: monogram+haiku vs baseline+haiku discrimination. HARD: crash is in a correct semaphore primitive touching a freed stack semaphore; the cause is the raw-mutex unlock waking a waiter that may already be gone on Py_PARK_INTR. Authored from PR #148852 fix diff (_PyRawMutex_LockSlow loops to Py_PARK_OK). NOTE: scaffold root_cause_file was the Misc/NEWS.d changelog .rst — corrected to Python/lock.c.

provenance: https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/148852 — gh-148820 _PyRawMutex UAF on spurious semaphore wakeup (merged 2026-04-22); fix commit ad3c5b7958b8; base 59b41c8c3ba3. Root cause + fix from the commit diff (_PyRawMutex_LockSlow loop until Py_PARK_OK; unsafe wakeup in _PyRawMutex_UnlockSlow).
